code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip().strip('"')

    # Special templates for predicates where the natural order differs from "subject verb object"
    special_templates = {
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the language of {s}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{clean(o)} is the capital of {s}",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"Construction of {s} began in {clean(o)}",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed in {clean(o)}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was inaugurated on {clean(o)}",
    }

    # Default verb map for predicates that keep the subject‑verb‑object order
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject unnecessarily
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        clauses = []

        # Preferred order for readability
        preferred = [
            "location", "height", "architect", "buildingType",
            "floorCount", "floorArea", "cost", "completionDate",
            "buildingStartDate", "inaugurationDate"
        ]

        handled = set()

        # Function to add a clause for a predicate, using special template if available
        def add_clause(pred):
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    if pred in special_templates:
                        clause = special_templates[pred](subject, tr.object)
                    else:
                        verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                        # Add noun for count‑type predicates
                        if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                            clause = f"{subject} {verb} {clean(tr.object)} floors"
                        else:
                            clause = f"{subject} {verb} {clean(tr.object)}"
                    clauses.append(clause)
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        for pred in preferred:
            add_clause(pred)

        # Add any remaining predicates not in the preferred list
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            if tr.predicate in special_templates:
                clause = special_templates[tr.predicate](subject, tr.object)
            else:
                verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
                if tr.predicate in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                    clause = f"{subject} {verb} {clean(tr.object)} floors"
                else:
                    clause = f"{subject} {verb} {clean(tr.object)}"
            clauses.append(clause)

        # Combine clauses into a single sentence for this subject
        if clauses:
            sentence = "; ".join(clauses) + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join sentences for different subjects
    final_text = " ".join(sentences).strip()
    # Capitalize first character
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
